diffCoverage.notes: Checked the send boundary, session reuse, and live-smoke output for leakage.

findings.evidence: send_message reuses an in-memory session keyed by account_id instead of accepting credentials from the UI; thread_id and text are validated (non-empty) at the boundary. The live-smoke sidecar was scanned and contains no credential values and no recipient phone number; only a message-id length is printed.
findings.fix: Add per-account rate limiting / send throttling before bulk or automated sends to reduce ban risk (project risk r1).

findings.evidence: send_text passes the message body straight to zca-rust send_message; no logging of message content. The recipient is addressed by an explicit thread_id, not a credential.
findings.fix: Keep message bodies out of any future debug logging.

summary: Security review of the send path: no credentials cross IPC (session reused from state), inputs validated at the boundary, and the attested live-smoke sidecar is free of secrets and the recipient phone number. Recommended (non-blocking) follow-up: per-account send throttling. No blocking findings.
